note: AI-facing feedback bank. Biased to DESCRIPTIVE (what happened / where it got in your way) over PRESCRIPTIVE (what to build). Grouped by theme so a batch can target what this session's metrics flagged. Grow toward ~100; keep each answerable in 5-10 sentences.

claim_check.q: When you claim-checked identifiers this session, did anything come back 'missing' or 'out_of_scope' that you actually expected the graph to know? Describe the case.
Did you ever proceed to write code referencing something claim-check flagged? If so, what made you override it?
Were there symbols you relied on that you didn't think to claim-check? What kind?

harness.q: Did the enforcement/harness ever block an edit you believed was legitimate? Describe what you were doing.
Did you ever work around Plexus (bypass, edit without consulting) this session? What pushed you to?
Was the consult-before-edit requirement ever unclear or in your way at a specific moment?

brief.q: Did the consultation brief give you what you needed before a change, or did you still have to go read files yourself? Which parts were missing?
Was anything in the brief misleading or stale relative to the actual code you then saw?
Was the brief the right length — too sparse to trust, or too long to be worth reading?

structure.q: Did the connectome seem to be missing nodes or relationships for the part of the codebase you worked on? What kind of thing was absent?
Did a simulation's predicted impact match what actually happened when you made the change? Where did it diverge?
Were any nodes classified into a region/type that felt wrong for what the code actually does?

general.q: At which single moment this session did Plexus help you most concretely?
At which single moment did it slow you down or add friction with no payoff?
If one thing about how Plexus works were different, which friction from THIS session would it have removed?
